title: Observation of topological superconductivity in a stoichiometric transition metal dichalcogenide 2M-WS2

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports ARPES, STM/STS, transport, and XRD studies of stoichiometric 2M-WS2, a layered transition-metal dichalcogenide with a superconducting transition at 8.8 K. The authors identify topological surface states and show that both the surface and bulk states develop a nodeless superconducting gap below Tc, supporting intrinsic topological superconductivity in a natural material.

figures.description: Conceptual schematic of how bulk s-wave superconductivity in 2M-WS2 induces superconductivity in topological surface states, enabling a 2D topological superconducting surface and possible Majorana modes at magnetic domain boundaries/vortices.
Crystal structure of 2M-WS2 showing the layered van der Waals structure, distorted octahedral coordination, and in-plane offsets between adjacent layers.

Transport and magnetization characterization: resistivity vs temperature showing sharp superconducting transition at 8.8 K, magnetization hysteresis loop at 2 K, and resistivity over 3–300 K with RRR = 152.
Schematic band structure in normal and superconducting states, illustrating bulk states and topological surface states and their superconducting gaps.
STM topography of the cleaved surface showing a defect-free topmost S-atom layer and comparison with ab initio charge-density simulation.
Bulk and surface Brillouin zone schematic with high-symmetry points and the ky-kz measurement plane indicated.
3D ARPES intensity plot around the Gamma point showing band dispersions and Fermi surfaces.
Comparison of experimental and calculated constant-energy contours at several binding energies.
Photon-energy-dependent ARPES map in the ky-kz plane showing kz dispersion and 3D electronic structure.
ARPES dispersions near the bulk Gamma point compared with calculations.
ARPES dispersions near the bulk Z point compared with calculations.
Laser-ARPES comparison between slab calculation and measured band dispersions along Y-Gamma-Y, highlighting the topological surface states near Gamma.
Zoomed-in TSS dispersion before and after in situ potassium dosing, showing an upward shift of the Fermi level and full visibility of the TSS.
Side-by-side comparison of TSS dispersions before and after potassium dosing, quantifying the ~45 meV Fermi-level shift.
Stacked experimental constant-energy contours near the Dirac point showing rapid evolution of the TSS.
Experimental constant-energy contours near the Dirac point.
Calculated constant-energy contours near the Dirac point for comparison with experiment.
ARPES spectra in superconducting and normal states showing superconducting gaps on both TSS and bulk states.
Temperature-dependent energy distribution curves at the Fermi momentum for TSS and bulk states, showing gap opening below Tc.
Temperature-dependent band dispersions across kF for TSS and bulk states, showing the superconducting gap evolution.
Extracted superconducting gap versus temperature for TSS and bulk states, compared with mean-field behavior.
Constant-energy contour showing kF loci used to probe angular dependence of the superconducting gap.
Angle-resolved EDCs at the superconducting state for TSS and bulk states, used to assess gap isotropy.
Superconducting gap magnitude versus angle for TSS and bulk states, showing nearly isotropic nodeless s-wave-like gaps.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include transport resistivity curves, magnetization hysteresis, STM topography scans, synchrotron ARPES spectra over multiple photon energies, laser ARPES spectra, and single-crystal XRD data. The paper states the samples were cleaved in situ for ARPES/STM and measured under ultra-high vacuum; ARPES data were collected with specific analyzers and energy/angle resolutions.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper presents processed ARPES intensity maps, constant-energy contours, kz-momentum maps, symmetrized spectra/EDCs, extracted superconducting gap values versus temperature and angle, and comparisons to ab initio calculations. Several plots are explicitly symmetrized with respect to ky=0 or EF and include fitted gap values with error bars.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The datasets that support the findings of this study are available from the corresponding author upon reasonable request. CCDC 1853656 contains the crystallographic data in the Supporting Information for this paper and can be obtained from the Cambridge Crystallographic Data Centre.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only at the instrument level: synchrotron ARPES from Elettra, Diamond I05, and SSRL BL5-2; laser ARPES at ShanghaiTech and Tsinghua; STM at cryogenic UHV; single-crystal XRD with Rigaku Oxford Diffraction; transport in PPMS. No raw file formats, folder structure, or repository are specified.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include symmetrized ARPES spectra, constant-energy contours, kz dispersion maps, extracted superconducting gaps, and fitted temperature/angle dependence. The paper also reports derived quantities such as RRR, Tc, and EF shift after potassium dosing.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: No explicit source-data package or figure-level data repository is mentioned in the text. Figure data appear to be embedded in the paper and supplementary figures, but availability of separate source data files is not stated.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are mentioned. First-principles calculations were performed with VASP, but no scripts are provided.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper does not state that source data files are deposited or that figure-level numerical data are provided. Data are only available on request, so the figures are not directly replot-ready from the paper text alone.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper distinguishes between experimental measurements and DFT calculations, but does not provide a public dataset structure. Supplementary figures are referenced for additional crystal structure, ARPES, STM, and gap-extraction details, yet no explicit source-data or raw-data archive is described.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: Methods list multiple direct measurements (ARPES, STM/STS, XRD, transport) and describe instrument settings, implying raw experimental acquisition.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper does not mention source-data files, repositories, or figure-specific data deposits; only availability on request is stated.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The datasets that support the findings of this study are available from the corresponding author upon reasonable request. CCDC 1853656 contains the crystallographic data...”
classification_prior.review_notes: Be careful not to infer public source-data availability from the presence of many figures and supplementary figures. The only explicit data-access statement is request-based access plus a CCDC crystallographic entry.
